ogTitle: Singleton Military Area vs Six Mile Swamp: Climate Safety

heading: Singleton Military Area vs Six Mile Swamp — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Singleton Military Area, New South Wales and Six Mile Swamp, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Six Mile Swamp has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Singleton Military Area (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Singleton Military Area or Six Mile Swamp safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Singleton Military Area has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Six Mile Swamp scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Singleton Military Area scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Six Mile Swamp scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Singleton Military Area or Six Mile Swamp?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Six Mile Swamp has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
